title: Jr. Web Developer
description: DailyPay is transforming the way people get paid. As a worktech company and the industry's leading on demand pay solution, DailyPay uses an award-winning technology platform to help America's top employers build stronger relationships with their employees. Reporting to the Senior Website Manager, the Junior Front-End Developer is responsible for supporting DailyPay's B2B and B2C marketing web ecosystem across WordPress (Elementor) and Contentful. Front-End Development & Customization: Build and maintain marketing pages across WordPress using Elementor and modular components in Contentful. Write custom CSS, JavaScript, and PHP to extend functionality beyond builder defaults. A/B Testing Implementation: Support the technical execution of experiments using CSS overrides, JavaScript DOM manipulation, and conditional rendering logic. Prevent layout shifts and flicker issues. ABM & Personalization Support: Assist in implementing account-based marketing experiences and custom audience journeys. Support integrations with ABM platforms. QA & Cross-Browser Testing: Own structured QA prior to launch, including responsive validation, form submission testing, link validation, tracking verification, and accessibility basics. Conduct thorough cross-browser testing across Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge, and mobile environments. Debugging & Technical Investigation: Use Chrome DevTools extensively to diagnose issues involving layout, performance, tracking, JavaScript conflicts, API/network calls, and third-party scripts. Performance & Technical Hygiene: Support optimization of page speed and Core Web Vitals. Ensure semantic HTML structure, manage CSS specificity cleanly, minimize unnecessary script load.
description_short: DailyPay is transforming the way people get paid. Junior Front-End Developer responsible for supporting B2B and B2C marketing web ecosystem across WordPress (Elementor) and Contentful. Build and maintain marketing pages, implement A/B tests, support ABM personalization, conduct QA and cross-browser testing, debug with Chrome DevTools, and optimize Core Web Vitals.

application_data.cover_letter:
Dear DailyPay Hiring Manager,

I am writing to express my interest in the Jr. Web Developer position at DailyPay. With hands-on experience in JavaScript, I am excited about the opportunity to contribute to your team.

I am particularly drawn to this role because of the remote flexibility, which allows me to bring my best work while maintaining a healthy work-life balance. My background in management has taught me the value of clear communication and taking initiative—qualities I bring to every project I work on.

What excites me about this position is the chance to grow my technical skills while contributing to meaningful work. I am a quick learner who thrives in environments where I can take ownership and solve problems independently.

I would welcome the chance to discuss how my skills and experience align with your needs. Thank you for considering my application.

Sincerely,
Tristan Carter

questions.question: On a scale of 1-10, how comfortable are you with Javascript and CSS? Briefly describe a project where you had to use this language to solve a specific UI or logic problem.
questions.answer: 10, I built my first three websites with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. I love creating unique designs and experiences. One example would be for my very first client project, I had built some cards displaying a photo of my client's work, with a description and I had given the cards a slick CSS :hover animation. Through manual testing, I found that CSS :hover doesn't have the best functionality  with touch devices. Instead of waiting for a tap to 'hover' the card, I used JavaScript to grab the scroll position, total card height, and the y-position of the top of the card. Then find an acceptable threshold to add/ remove a class based on if the card was passed that threshold or not. Essentially, I added a class when the card was on-screen to mimic the hover effect.

questions.question: What tools do you use when you are stuck on a problem.
questions.answer: If I can't immediately solve a bug and I know that it is something I'm uncomfortable with. I will sic copilot or, if it's connected, my Hermes agent to triage, but not edit code. I will have it list out possible root causes and investigate each root cause to see which has a higher chance of being the real issue. Also Sentry's Seer tool is great at pin-pointing root cause of issues.
